title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
summary: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems.

sections.overview: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploitation of three vulnerabilities that are codenamed BlueHammer (requires GitHub sign-in), RedSun, and UnDefend, all of which were released as zero-days by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse (
sections.technical: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploitation of three vulnerabilities that are codenamed BlueHammer (requires GitHub sign-in), RedSun, and UnDefend, all of which were released as zero-days by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse (
sections.impact:
Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems.

Assessed severity: CRITICAL.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Affected users should rotate credentials, enable MFA, and monitor accounts for suspicious activity.
sections.context:
Zero-day vulnerabilities represent the highest-priority threats as they are exploited before vendors can issue patches. Rapid detection and compensating controls are essential.

Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Data breaches carry significant regulatory, financial, and reputational consequences. Affected organizations and individuals should monitor for follow-on attacks using exposed data.

email_summary: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. Severity is rated critical.

title: Google Blocks 8.3B Policy-Violating Ads in 2025, Launches Android 17 Privacy Overhaul
summary: Google this week announced a new set of Play policy updates to strengthen user privacy and protect businesses against fraud, even as it revealed it blocked or removed over 8.3 billion ads globally and suspended 24.9 million accounts in 2025.

sections.overview: Google this week announced a new set of Play policy updates to strengthen user privacy and protect businesses against fraud, even as it revealed it blocked or removed over 8.3 billion ads globally and suspended 24.9 million accounts in 2025. title: Coast Guard's New Cybersecurity Rules Offers Lessons for CISOs
summary: The Maritime Transportation Security Act (MTSA) requires plans to protect OT systems, audits by independent third parties, and a hybrid OT-security role.

title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability Database (NVD), stating it will only enrich those that fulfil certain conditions owing to an explosion in CVE submissions.

title: Operation PowerOFF Seizes 53 DDoS Domains, Exposes 3 Million Criminal Accounts
summary: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more than 75,000 cybercriminals.

sections.overview: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more than 75,000 cybercriminals. The ongoing effort, dubbed Operation PowerOFF, disrupted access to the DDoS-for-hire services, took down the technical infrastructure supporting them, and obtained access to
sections.technical: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more than 75,000 cybercriminals.
sections.impact:
Assessed severity: MEDIUM.

Systems running affected software versions are at risk until patches or mitigations are applied.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
summary: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

sections.overview: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To that end, the agency has added the vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-34197 (CVSS score: 8.8), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian

title: Webinar: From phishing to fallout — Why MSPs must rethink both security and recovery
summary: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and recovery strategies to reduce risk and maintain business continuity. [...]

sections.overview: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and recovery strategies to reduce risk and maintain business continuity. [...]
sections.technical: This involves malicious software designed to compromise, exfiltrate, or disrupt targeted systems. The attack leverages social engineering techniques to trick users into revealing credentials or executing malicious payloads.
sections.mitigation:
Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime.

Reinforce security awareness training. Ensure email filtering, link scanning, and multi-factor authentication are enabled. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Phishing remains the most common initial access vector. Security awareness training combined with technical controls like email filtering and MFA are key defenses.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

email_summary: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and recovery strategies to reduce risk and maintain business continuity. [...].
